message.content:
I had this error:

test webhooks::tests::sealed_vault_rejects_plaintext_secret_updates ... ok

  failures:

  ---- methods::services::tests::memory_config_get_reports_typed_memory_fields stdout ----

  thread 'methods::services::tests::memory_config_get_reports_typed_memory_fields' (203545233) panicked at crates/gateway/src/methods/
  services.rs:5269:9:
  assertion `left == right` failed
    left: String("prompt-only")
   right: "search-only"
  note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace


  failures:
      methods::services::tests::memory_config_get_reports_typed_memory_fields

  test result: FAILED. 333 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 1.44s

  error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p moltis-gateway --lib`
  error: process didn't exit successfully: `/Users/penso/.rustup/toolchains/nightly-2025-11-30-aarch64-apple-darwin/bin/cargo test --tests --manifest-
  path /Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/Cargo.toml --target-dir /Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/target/llvm-cov-target --workspace` (exit status: 101)
  [local/coverage] failed in 146s
  ~/t/m/moltis main [⇣] ❯

I've made changes to fix it, can you confirm that's the right way?

content.text: Let me look at the failing test and your changes.

input.description: Show staged and unstaged changes to services.rs

content.text: The failure is a test isolation issue — `payload["style"]` returns `"prompt-only"` instead of `"search-only"`, meaning another test was mutating the shared config concurrently. Let me check the lock you're switching to.
Let me see if other test modules also use this shared lock.

input.description: Show changes to other modified files

content.text:
Yes, this is the right fix. The problem was that three test modules (`services.rs`, `local_llm_setup.rs`, `server.rs`) each had their own **independent** `Mutex<()>` to serialize config-mutating tests. Since each `static LOCK` lived in a different module, tests across modules could still run concurrently and stomp on each other's config — which is exactly why `memory_config_get_reports_typed_memory_fields` saw `"prompt-only"` (from a concurrent test) instead of the `"search-only"` it set up.

Your fix consolidates all three into a single `crate::config_override_test_lock()` in `lib.rs`, so **all** config-mutating tests across the gateway crate share the same mutex. Clean approach.

One minor note: `server.rs` and `local_llm_setup.rs` used `.unwrap()` on the lock, while the new shared function uses `.unwrap_or_else(|error| error.into_inner())` (poison recovery) — that's actually an improvement per the project's "never `.unwrap()` in production" guideline (and more robust if a test panics).

Want me to run the test to confirm it passes now?

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(gateway): unify config-override test lock to prevent flaky test

Each test module had its own static Mutex for serializing
config-mutating tests, allowing cross-module races. Consolidate
into a single crate-level lock so all gateway config tests serialize
properly.
EOF
)"
